articles.title: CBSE vs student: Board admits flaws after teen hacker exposes website vulnerabilities
articles.description: CBSE has addressed security vulnerabilities found in its OnMark portal, thanking ethical hackers for their assistance. A cybersecurity team, including experts from government agencies and IITs, has been deployed to strengthen the system. The statement comes after 19-year-old ethical hacker Nisarga Adhikary alleged security flaws in CBSE's digital evaluation ecosystem

articles.title: 'Insanely insecure': Ethical hacker alleges CBSE answer sheets, question papers were publicly accessible
articles.description: CBSE people didn't configure their AWS bucket properly, and now we can paginate and enumerate all their media, which has 2026 answer sheets and question papers, claims Nisarga

articles.title: Canada tightens documentation rules for digital nomad workers
articles.description: Canada has tightened documentation rules for digital nomads entering under a work-permit exemption. Applicants must now prove their income is earned entirely outside Canada and they work remotely for foreign entities. Immigration officers will verify these conditions to ensure digital nomads do not enter the Canadian labor market.

articles.title: 'Insane bubble and bigger than 1999': Zoho founder Sridhar Vembu has a scary warning about the future of AI
articles.description: Zoho founder Sridhar Vembu warns of a potential market bubble driven by AI enthusiasm, citing high price-to-sales ratios for tech giants like Nvidia, Apple, and Microsoft. He argues these valuations are disconnected from fundamentals, drawing parallels to the dot-com era and suggesting the current bubble could be even larger.

articles.title: Nigerian Boy Makes Washing Machine From Cardboard That Actually Works, Stuns Internet (WATCH)
articles.description: A Nigerian boy has captured the internet’s attention after building a fully functional miniature washing machine using nothing more than cardboard, plastic bottles, and a handful of everyday materials, proving it worked by washing a piece of cloth on camera.

articles.title: India poised to benefit as global wealth turns to AI, infra, emerging markets: Report
articles.description: Global family offices are increasingly directing capital toward artificial intelligence, infrastructure, energy transition and emerging markets as they reposition portfolios for a more uncertain world. A new UBS report suggests these trends could create significant opportunities for India across technology, infrastructure, private markets and gold investments.

articles.title: AUKUS deal streamlined: Australia to receive used US nuclear powered submarines for cost-effectiveness
articles.description: Australia will now receive three used Virginia-class nuclear-powered submarines from the United States. This change streamlines the AUKUS security pact. Defence Minister Richard Marles stated the move is cost-effective. The submarines will be in-service vessels from the US Navy stock. This decision simplifies supply chain and maintenance. The AUKUS submarine program is a significant part of Australia's defense strategy.

articles.title: Upcoming Smartphones in June 2026: Motorola Edge 70 Pro+, Xiaomi 17T, Lava Bold N2 5G and More
articles.description: Brands like Motorola, Huawei, and Xiaomi are set to unveil new devices across flagship, mid-range, and budget segments in June this year. Here we have listed the smartphone launches set to take place next month. Our picks include the Xiaomi 17T, Motorola Edge 70 Pro+, Lava Bold N2 5, and Huawei Nova 16 series, among others.

This 24/7 assistant automates tasks across Google Workspace and connected apps, working in the background to manage emails, plan trips, and even browse the web remotely.

That’s a bit less than the A37 discount, but it’s enough to shift the balance of power between the two. The A37 is the cheaper model and if you can live with 6GB of RAM and 128GB non-expandable storage, then yes, it is meaningfully cheaper. But the 256GB models of the A37 and A57 are currently only $10 apart. Samsung Galaxy A57 $60 off Read our review ...

articles.title: Nvidia CFO Colette Kress at company's earnings call: AI is no longer nice-to-have as ...
articles.description: Nvidia's CFO, Colette Kress, declared AI a business necessity, not a luxury, driving demand across the AI stack. The chip giant is restructuring its reporting into Data Center and Edge Computing segments to reflect this growth.

articles.title: Indian cuts export duties on petrol, diesel and aviation turbine fuel
articles.description: India will cut its export duty on petrol diesel and aviation turbine fuel (ATF) for the fortnight starting June 1, its government said in a statement on Saturday.

articles.title: Kick’s new feature lets streamers hide live viewer numbers from streams
articles.description: Kick now lets streamers hide live viewer counts, a move aiming to ease pressure on creators, especially smaller ones. This optional feature shifts focus from raw popularity, potentially influencing how streams are recommended. While supporters hail it as a boost for new talent, critics worry about losing a gauge of trending content.

articles.title: Mystery Beats headphones appear around Lamine Yamal's neck
articles.description: A new pair of Beats headphones are on the way, as a new unnamed model surfaces in a series of Instagram posts by football star Lamine Yamal. A still from a video showing the new unannounced Beats headphones - Image Credit: Lamine Yamal On May 23, some mystery headphones appeared in an FCC filing , indicating a new pair was coming out of Cupertino. One week later, an Instagram post has shown that it's from Beats. The shots on the official account for Lamine Yamal showed the sporting celebrity wearing and carrying around some bright pink headphones. In the four photographs and one video, the headphones are either around his neck or hanging off a bag to his side. Continue Reading on AppleInsider | Discuss on our Forums

articles.title: Indie App Spotlight: ‘NextThere’ helps you navigate public transit with rich insights
articles.description: Welcome to Indie App Spotlight . This is a weekly 9to5Mac series where we showcase the latest apps in the indie app world. If you’re a developer and would like your app featured, get in contact . Navigating across different cities and figuring out their public transportation can sometimes be a bit messy. Maps apps might not always have the best information, or the apps that do exist aren’t presenting data in a super readable manner. NextThere aims to tackle that problem, and also packs loads of historical data for providing more informed information regarding delays, on-time performance, and so on. It’s great for tourists and frequent commuters alike. more...

articles.description: Over 120 Indian deeptech startups will showcase their innovations at the Bharat Innovates event in Nice, France, from June 14-16. This initiative, part of the India-France year of Innovation, aims to foster global partnerships and attract investment for India's emerging tech ecosystem. The event will highlight advancements across 13 frontier technology themes.
